title: Modern Factions – Organizations
description: Contemporary civic, criminal, nonprofit, and informal organizations for modern stories and investigations.

entries.result: The neighborhood watch coalition protects its members from outside scrutiny while presenting itself as civic-minded; Their help can open doors, but their expectations travel with it.
The neighborhood watch coalition knows the city’s unofficial routes, favors, and grudges; They know where the paper trail begins and where it gets buried.
The neighborhood watch coalition keeps careful records and shares them only with people who prove useful; They are friendly until they feel used.
The neighborhood watch coalition claims to serve the public but often protects its own first; Crossing them creates social consequences before legal ones.
The neighborhood watch coalition trades in favors rather than money and remembers every slight; Their enemies describe them as meddling, corrupt, or necessary.
The neighborhood watch coalition has one legitimate arm and one deniable arm that handles uglier errands; They can provide access, witnesses, vehicles, or pressure.
The neighborhood watch coalition operates through potlucks, text chains, and quiet pressure; They fear exposure almost as much as failure.
The neighborhood watch coalition collects secrets because secrets are more dependable than donations; Their internal dispute may become the real obstacle.
The neighborhood watch coalition believes the authorities are too slow and acts before asking permission; They measure trust in repeated small favors.
The neighborhood watch coalition is split between older members seeking control and younger members demanding transparency; They are strongest on familiar streets and weakest under scrutiny.
